# PolySimulator API

> Virtual trading environment for Polymarket prediction markets.

PolySimulator provides a complete HFT API for algorithmic trading bots to practice trading with real market data using simulated money, then switch to live trading on Polymarket by changing only API credentials.

## API Base URL
- Production: https://api.polysimulator.com/v1
- Local: http://localhost:8000/v1

## Authentication
All endpoints (except bootstrap) require an API key via `X-API-Key` header.
Key format: `ps_live_` prefix + 64 hex characters (total 72 chars).

### First-time setup (Bootstrap)
New users have no key yet. To create your first key without a chicken-and-egg problem:
1. Sign in at polysimulator.com to get a Supabase access_token.
2. Call `POST /v1/keys/bootstrap` with `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.
3. Store the returned `raw_key` — it is shown only once.
4. All subsequent requests use `X-API-Key: <raw_key>`.

Manage additional keys at POST /v1/keys, GET /v1/keys, DELETE /v1/keys/{key_id}.

## Rate Limits
- Free: 5 req/sec, 300 req/min, 2 WebSocket connections, batch size 5
- Pro: 20 req/sec, 1,200 req/min, 10 WebSocket connections, batch size 20
- Enterprise: 50 req/sec, 3,000 req/min, 50 WebSocket connections, batch size 50
Rate limit headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset

## Key Concepts
- All numeric values (price, quantity, balance) are JSON strings to avoid IEEE 754 precision loss
- Use Decimal types in your language for arithmetic
- Use `client_order_id` in order requests to prevent duplicate fills on retries (idempotency)
- Markets use condition_id as the primary identifier

## Endpoints

### Market Data
- GET /v1/markets — List active markets (params: hot_only, limit, offset, search)
- GET /v1/markets/{condition_id} — Get single market with live price
- GET /v1/markets/{condition_id}/book — L2 order book (bids/asks arrays)
- GET /v1/markets/{condition_id}/candles — OHLCV price candles (intervals: 1m, 5m, 15m, 1h, 4h, 1d)
- POST /v1/prices/batch — Batch price lookup (body: {"market_ids": [...]}, max 50; returns flat: condition_id, buy, sell, outcomes[], source)

### Trading
- POST /v1/clob/order — Place CLOB-compatible order (body: token_id, side, size, price, order_type, client_order_id — price and size are strings)
- GET /v1/orders — List orders (params: status, market_id, side, limit, offset, cursor)
- DELETE /v1/orders/{order_id} — Cancel open order
- POST /v1/orders/batch — Place multiple orders (body: {"orders": [...]})

### Account
- GET /v1/account/balance — Account balance, PnL, unrealized PnL
- GET /v1/account/positions — Open/closed positions (params: status, market_id, limit, offset)
- GET /v1/account/portfolio — Aggregate portfolio snapshot
- GET /v1/account/history — Filled order history (params: market_id, side, limit, offset, cursor)
- GET /v1/account/equity — Hourly equity snapshots (params: start, end, interval)

### Key Management
- POST /v1/keys/bootstrap — Create first API key via Bearer JWT (no X-API-Key needed)
- POST /v1/keys — Create additional API key (body: name, tier, permissions; requires X-API-Key)
- GET /v1/keys — List all keys (masked)
- DELETE /v1/keys/{key_id} — Revoke key
- GET /v1/keys/tiers — List available rate limit tiers
- POST /v1/keys/ws-token — Get short-lived WebSocket auth token

### Health & Monitoring
- GET /v1/health — Liveness probe
- GET /v1/health/ready — Readiness probe (checks DB + Redis)
- GET /v1/status — System status with market sync info

### WebSocket
- WS /v1/ws/prices?token=<jwt> — Real-time price stream
  - Auth: pass JWT from POST /v1/keys/ws-token as query param
  - Subscribe: {"action": "subscribe", "markets": ["cond-123", ...]}
  - Unsubscribe: {"action": "unsubscribe", "markets": ["cond-123"]}
  - Ping: {"action": "ping"} → {"type": "pong"}
  - Price message: {"type": "price", "market_id": "cond-123", "buy": "0.65", "sell": "0.35", "best_bid": "0.64", "best_ask": "0.66", "volume": "1000.00", "source": "clob", "updated_at": "..."}
- WS /v1/ws/executions?token=<jwt> — Trade fill notifications
- POST /v1/keys/ws-token — Mint short-lived WebSocket auth token (60s TTL)

### CLOB Compatibility
- POST /v1/clob/order — Polymarket CLOB-compatible order endpoint
  - Same schema as Polymarket's POST /order — change only the URL and auth header to go live
  - All numeric fields (price, size) must be strings: "0.65", not 0.65
  - Response mirrors Polymarket: {success, orderID, status, price, size, side, takingAmount, makingAmount}

## Order Types & Time-in-Force
- POST /v1/orders uses two fields:
  - order_type: "market" (fill at current price) or "limit" (execute at specified price or better)
  - time_in_force: "GTC" (good-til-cancel, default) or "IOC" (immediate-or-cancel)
- POST /v1/clob/order uses order_type for time-in-force policy (matching Polymarket's CLOB schema):
  - "GTC" (good-til-cancelled, default), "FOK" (fill-or-kill), "IOC" (immediate-or-cancel), "GTD" (good-til-date)
  - All CLOB orders are effectively limit orders (price is required)
- Side values are always uppercase: "BUY" or "SELL"

## Error Format
{"error": "ERROR_CODE", "message": "Human description", "details": {...}}

## Common Errors
- 400 INSUFFICIENT_BALANCE — Not enough funds
- 400 MARKET_CLOSED — Market is resolved
- 401 INVALID_API_KEY — Key invalid or revoked
- 404 MARKET_NOT_FOUND — Unknown market
- 404 ORDER_NOT_FOUND — Unknown order
- 409 SLIPPAGE_EXCEEDED — Price moved beyond max_slippage_bps
- 429 RATE_LIMITED — Too many requests (check Retry-After header)

## Trading Mode
- Virtual (default): All trades are simulated with $1,000 starting balance
- Live: Same API, same code — swap base URL to `https://clob.polymarket.com` and use Polymarket's official auth headers (`POLY_API_KEY`, `POLY_API_SECRET`, `POLY_PASSPHRASE`)

## Links
- Documentation: https://docs.polysimulator.com
- GitHub: https://github.com/Bavariance/polysimulator
- Website: https://polysimulator.com
